mission.title: kuma: Feature Request: Filter Envoy Metrics
mission.description: Feature Request: Filter Envoy Metrics. Requested by 7+ users.

steps.title: Check current kuma deployment
steps.description:
Verify your kuma version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kuma -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kuma
helm list -n kuma 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working kuma installation.

steps.title: Review kuma configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kuma configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kuma -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kuma
kubectl get configmap -n kuma -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kuma
```
The default metrics generated by envoy proxy is relatively big. In a kubernetes cluster with around 200 services inside the kuma mesh, a single call to `/metrics` resulted in around 7MB of metrics data.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Feature Request: Filter Envoy Metrics
steps.description:
### Summary

The Envoy Prometheus endpoint supports passing a filtering regex in
the query parameters to limit the set of metrics that are returned.
This feature lets a metrics scraper scrape different sets of metrics
at different intervals.

### Full changelog

* Pass query parameters through the
```yaml
---
apiVersion: kuma.io/v1alpha1
kind: Mesh
metadata:
  name: default
spec:
  metrics:
    backends:
    - conf:
        path: /metrics
        port: 5670
        filter: 'envoy_cluster_*' # Only include metrics that matches this pattern
        tags:
          kuma.io/service: dataplane-metrics
      name: prometheus-1
      type: prometheus
    enabledBackend: prometheus-1
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kuma -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kuma
kubectl get events -n kuma --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Feature Request: Filter Envoy Metrics" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
### Summary

The Envoy Prometheus endpoint supports passing a filtering regex in
the query parameters to limit the set of metrics that are returned.
This feature lets a metrics scraper scrape different sets of metrics
at different intervals.

### Full changelog

* Pass query parameters through the DP metrics endpoint so that metrics scrapers can sample subsets of the available metrics.
codeSnippets:
---
apiVersion: kuma.io/v1alpha1
kind: Mesh
metadata:
  name: default
spec:
  metrics:
    backends:
    - conf:
        path: /metrics
        port: 5670
        filter: 'envoy_cluster_*' # Only include metrics that matches this pattern
        tags:
          kuma.io/service: dataplane-metrics
      name: prometheus-1
      type: prometheus
    enabledBackend: prometheus-1

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kuma installed or the issue environment reproducible.
